frontier-paradigm-shifts.title: Paradigm Shifts
frontier-paradigm-shifts.lens: The portfolio of evidence • The concept of distribution • Recourseable research
frontier-paradigm-shifts.intro: A source-led atlas of Paradigm Shifts, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
frontier-paradigm-shifts.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Paradigm Shifts.
deepDive: The hotspots were judged to be five dimensions: recent peer review publication and links to topics, research centres/projects/data infrastructure, methodological innovations, cross-regional proliferation, and public issues. The internal code for each 0-2 is used only to identify the relative shapes in the evidence pool of this report, which cannot be added across regions or extrapolated from the “world ranking”. The ongoing meetings, conferences and visits in 2026 are only going to the "front radar"; the final complete trend year is 2025.

The “five-fold shift” below is an explanatory composite proposition of cross-regional evidence, not a statistical fact obtained mechanically by the five indicators, nor does it claim that all countries, languages and schools are moving in tandem. The minimum conditions for their establishment are: reciprocal change of object, reorganization of methods or dispute of responsibility in at least two regions and two types of evidence; and the retention of the reverse and uncovered areas by the regional chapters and chapter 24. The role of the framework is thus to compare the organization with the presentation of rebuttable questions, rather than to declare that the uniform paradigm has been completed.

This distinction is particularly important as the metadata platform systematically rewards English, DOI, open abstract and large institutions. Open Alex is an open and recapable academic index, but its coverage and conceptual recognition are still influenced by sources and languages; the trend map that follows in this report is based on an answer to “How much is a concept label in English visible in the title/summary” and not “how many studies are there in the world”. (Priem et al. 2022)
coreIdeas: Targeting amendments: Extension of printed text to platforms, models, voices, clinical and environmental infrastructure.

Geo-revision: expansion of theoretical production from the Euro-American Centre to Latin America, Africa, Asia, the Middle East and Central and Eastern Europe, with records translated and untranslated.
Methodological amendments: Require conceptual and negative nuclear materials, data spectrum and ethical decision-making.
Convert the portfolio of evidence into an observable and questionable analytical dimension.
method: Describe research issues, location and access to materials.
Sets a scale correspondence between reading, computing or experiment.
The interpretation of constraints is verified by case, reverse and triangulation.
Open uncertainty, ethical boundaries and the path to recovery.
cases: Did the new concept provide the mechanisms for interpretation that had previously been lacking? |AI-Mundo, hydrocolonialism, unruly coparison + original text + at least one subsequent adoption/disputing signal
Why did the old theory revive the classic concept of the classic and regain its interpretation under new conditions? The Reinterpretation of New Object/Methods in the 2020s

Has the method of migration changed the question that can be asked when it enters literary research? LLM Memory Audit, Text Re-use Network, Eye and Long Text Reading Method Note + Error/Emporal Border + Literature Interpretation Reread

Are the institutional agenda periodicals, meetings, funding and platforms being restructured to study ecology? Multilingual infrastructure, emergency digital humanities, critical medical humanities signals of at least two types; meetings may not be separately mature degrees

frontier-methodology-map.title: Methodological Atlas
frontier-methodology-map.intro: A source-led atlas of Methodological Atlas, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
frontier-methodology-map.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Methodological Atlas.
deepDive: John Guillory defines reading in detail (*On Close Reading*) as “cultural technology”: researchers can demonstrate how to take note of words, phrases, quotations, formal relationships and context, or can train such attention without exhausting judgement as a set of mechanical rules; Scott Newstok, author's public signature, prepares an annotated bibliography for the book and not a non-differentiated co-author. The criticisms were further reversible as verifiable writing actions: accuracy of quotations, fairness of interpretation, factual summary of selectivity, dispositiveness of narrative judgement supported by text details, and genuine handling of retrogressive examples.

This triangle amends the pseudo-relationship of “detailed-calculation”: reading provides a local judgement that can be argued publicly, and computing provides a measure, distribution and anomaly, both of which must demonstrate a chain of reasoning from material to claim. They cannot introduce “detailed reading as the only way” or miswritten literary judgements as a complete replica of the natural sciences; the more appropriate criteria are availability of the version, availability of evidence, exemplifying steps, retrogression, reasonable peer review.

(c) Identify the target, the scale, the language, the time and the location of the researcher. The study of “Asian literature” must describe how Asia is defined; the study of Russian-language works cannot automatically be classified as Russian literature; and the study of indigenous materials should describe the relationship between the researcher and the community. The question should be answered by evidence, rather than packaging a grand value judgement into a search mission.
coreIdeas: Language material: Clarity total, sampling, copyright, language, version, quality of OCR, age and gender/geographical deficit.
Features: An explanation of why it is literary and does not replace the theory with model convenience.
Validation: Language baseline, set-up, parameter sensitivity, manual blinding, abnormal value back.
Explanation: Distinguishing related, classification performance, mechanisms and historical causes and consequences; prohibition of direct naming of genres by a cluster.
method: The primary question of methodology is not “what software is used”, but “what is the basis for this conclusion from these materials”. The methodology is understood in the present report as a contract between the claim and the evidence. Any study should answer four questions: What is the research unit? How does the material get into the sample? How can the analytical steps change the material? What result would overturn the explanation?
Figure 3 Materials-scale-validation relationships for reading, archiving, computing, experimentation, participatory and media methods
Reads the book of knowledge: the skills that can be displayed, not the mysterious instinct or the fixed algorithm

John Guillory defines reading in detail (*On Close Reading*) as “cultural technology”: researchers can demonstrate how to take note of words, phrases, quotations, formal relationships and context, or can train such attention without exhausting judgement as a set of mechanical rules; Scott Newstok, author's public signature, prepares an annotated bibliography for the book and not a non-differentiated co-author.
cases: How to use the narrative, the rhetoric, the rhythm, the sound and the ambiguity of the work, the version, the translation of the text, the counter-case, the speech and language skills, the overall frequency or causal effect of the area

The historical archives/book history concept, the classics, the author and publishing system, how to form the complete experience of groups outside the archives, the manuscripts, the letters, the catalogues, the newspapers, the translation library selection, the missing and the statement of archival authority
How to change the original language/translation, book reviews, courses, publication chain logs, translation spectrum, multilingual team unsupported “global impact”

Calculate the historical significance and value of text/networking patterns, how anomalies and relationships are distributed at larger scales, structured text, metadata, web-based text spectrum, language validation, parameter scanning, code version |-related models

digital-humanities.title: Digital Humanities
digital-humanities.intro: A source-led atlas of Digital Humanities, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
digital-humanities.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Digital Humanities.
deepDive: Globalization has also shifted from “transplanting English tools to the world” to multilingual OCR, local knowledge mapping, indigenous data governance, infrastructure and inequality in the South. Global Data in the Digital Humanities put African, Latin American, South Asian, China and Russian connections in the Centre for Theory; Data Feminism requires auditing data projects from power, binary classification, missing data, labour and co-design (Fiormonte et al., 2022; D'Ignazio et al., 2020).

Application issues: Long-term changes in body structure, proliferation of types, translation networks, person relations, periodical ecology, spatial narratives, variations in versions. Not applicable/high risk: language is not known in general but claims to be “representative of all literature”; very small language uses high-parametric models; no cross-language comparison by language specialists; protected text is reissued. The "Calculating Discovery - Archiving - Detailed Interpretation" triangle is preferred.

Conceptual distinction. “distant reading” means observing distribution, variation and anomalies in the scale of material beyond the read-in-the-post capability; “computational literature studies” further uses classification, measurement, network or semantic models for literary issues; and “critical digital humanities” asks why the text exists, who is digitalized and who sets the classification criteria. The three cannot be compressed to "reading in software." Core mechanisms. Ted Underwood, in *Distant Horizons: Digital Experience and Literary Change*, interprets the model as a “view device”: researchers first turn literary history into an observable feature, then examine it in different language, parameters and time-slice spectrometry, and then return to the missing interpretation of the work, publication history and archives. The value of the model is not to pronounce meaning on behalf of others, but to expose a certain historical intuition to the opposite.
coreIdeas: Language is not neutral: the digital collection is a product of choice, copyright, commercial cooperation, preservation and OCR performance. Beelen and others presented the Environmental Scan in the British press archives and opposed the Panorama (Beelen et al., 2025) by “oligoptic data”.

How the results of the model become literary evidence: high classification accuracy only supports the scoring of characteristics, and does not automatically indicate their literary historical significance. Qualified papers are to report baselines, time/author confusion, data leakage, category imbalances, text errors and alternative theories (Da, 2019; Bode, 2023; Hicke, etc., 2025).

Uncertainty is not about cleaning noise: Undate retains uncertainty about human time in spatial, multi-chronological and incomplete dates, and demonstrates “informing theoretical judgement into data structures and publicly describing” (Koeser et al., 2025).

Interpretable and memory pollution: LLM may learn both style and popular text. The hint source identification, training, text disturbance and internal detection must be combined, and the black box accuracy cannot be called “understanding” (Hicke et al., 2025).
method: Language protocol: Time, language, type, sub-band, copyright, expected aggregate and digital coverage; source and version reserved for each text.

Exploration and modelling: The word frequency/key is the starting point, can be measured in a stylistic manner, subject/input, supervised classification, person network, geographic information and serial model; simple baseline and time-specification is required.
Validation: Cross-certification divided by work/author rather than by random sentence; label replacement, digestion, artificial blind core, sensitivity analysis and negative cases.
Returning to literature: extracting text from model defects and anomalies, reading their rhetoric, style and publishing context; translating “statistical differences” into rebuttable literary titles.
cases: Ted Underwood makes televised evidence an issue of literary change rather than just displaying tools (Underwood, 2019) The English publication of historical examples cannot directly represent global literature.

The authors of the 80-year-old Minister of the Interior, Hicke & Mimno/Cornell, 27 authors, belong to 30 stylistics; compare SVM, Delta and LLM and do memory, disturbance and detection (Hicke et al., 2025) stylization is age-relevant; the effect of the hot-door author memory is evident

Beelen et al./University of London, Alan Turing Institute and others, split the missing into cases of coverage, divergence, partisanship, and set up reference metadata calibration (Beelen et al., 2025) Not all files are available in the English press.

Koeser et al./Princeton, ASU, MPIWG, Harvey |undate makes the fuzzy date, multi-chronology and inter-section reasoning reusable Python pack (Koeser et al., 2025) lymph software is still evolving; the routing does not eliminate the uncertainty of the historical material itself

ai-literature.title: AI and Literature
ai-literature.intro: A source-led atlas of AI and Literature, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
ai-literature.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of AI and Literature.
deepDive: Areas of application: Generating literary history, computational narratives, translation collaboration, writing processes, literature education, author rights and platform governance. Primary limitations: The non-recurrence of model updates; the lack of transparency in training packages; the bias of English/high resource languages; the drift of evaluation concepts; and rapid policy change. The findings must be modelled and dated, preferably with outputs and tips as auditable appendices.

Conceptual distinction. The AI literature research consists of at least three objects: models as a label, translation or comparison tool; models as subjects of research to carry training language deviations and cultural orthodox; and models as a participatory device in the creative process. Combining the three into “can the machine write” would obscure a truly researchable mechanism. Core mechanisms. In “Human and Machine Cultures of Reaing: A Cognitive-Assemblage Approach”, Katherine Hayles proposed “cognitive assembly”: production of significance is distributed among people, interfaces, algorithms, systems and the environment. Thus, the production is not a single-subject expression, but rather a result of the continuous role of training materials, model structures, system tips, tips, candidates, manual editing, signature, publication and marketing. The author's analysis should record who makes the expressive decision at every point, not just the “person-written” or “AI-written”.

Dennis Yi Tenen, The Theory of Robotics: How Computers Learn for Writers: How Computers Lineed to Writer*, explains that “mechanical writing” does not suddenly start with a large-language model before templates, rhetorical combinations, formalism and early calculations are brought to the production model. (Tenen, 2024) Matthew G. Kirschenbaum, Official Academic Signature, Revising the Traces: A Literary History of Word Processing*, incorporates document formats, software interfaces, revised trajectory and digital author files into the literary history, which can alter the preservation, visible and attributable process, but cannot be the basis for the individual attribution of text changes to a particular software.
coreIdeas: The author ' s identity has changed from duality to a contribution spectrum: pure human, pure machine, mixed only with a rough label; and more importantly, who controls the conception, hint, selection, translation, rewrite, sorting and publication. The United States Copyright Agency considers that the ancillary use of AI under current United States law does not prevent the original parts of humanity from being protected, but pure AI material or material under which humans have inadequate control over the expression element is not protected; mere provision of tips is usually insufficient (U.S. Copyright Office, 2025). This is evidence of United States policy and should not be extrapolated to a globally uniform law.

Generating abilities and narrative understandings cannot be confused: Tian, for example, uses storytelling, turning points, awakening and value-for-money comparison of human stories, and finds that LLM stories in tests are more homogenous in direction, suspense and structural diversity; and visible integration feature can significantly improve indicators (Tian et al., 2024). This indicates that the middle-level narrative is valid and does not prove that the machine has human experience.

The automatic evaluation cycle deviation: Chhun and others found in 1,056 stories and multiple tips that LLM ratings are better than old automatic indicators at the system level, but that they are difficult to provide sufficient and consistent interpretation; models may also prefer text that is closer to pre-trained distribution (Chhun and others, 2024). Therefore, homologue models alone cannot be used to generate and score.

Collaboration is not automatic: Teleki and others distinguish between two paradigms of " independent generation " and " author-assisted " (Teleki and others, 2025). The 41 blind reviews of the UCB 2024 contest show that four pure AI works are in the middle and the winners work together; some collaboration takes longer (Park, 2026). This is an exploratory case and cannot be declared a general superiority for mixed writing.
method: Freeze model name, version, provider, date, temperature, Top-p, system program, context and random torrent; save all hints-output-manual editing logs.

The narrative studies add mid-level labels: event, cause, effect, turn, focus, target, suspense, emotional trajectory; and which level of improvement is the result of profiling validation (Tian et al., 2024).
The retention of the creative research versions differs from AI recommendations, author adoption, refusal to rewriting; the cognitive labour and power relations of interview writing authors.
Open uncertainty, ethical boundaries and the path to recovery.
cases: LLM can recognize short clips? |Hicke & Mimno (Hicke et al., 2025) | specific models, textures, tasks above random/traditional baselines; useful | models such as word lines and prostheses understand styles as humans; result is multi-linguistic

LLM stories differ from human stories in the Tian et al. (2024) |ssail, structure, value differences in test text; language constraints improve the |sace of human stories forever; automatic indicators are equivalent to aesthetic values

LLM could be useful in replacing the Humans Committee |chhun et al., 2024 | system ranking; the explanation is still poor, and human verification of the | can eliminate human evaluation or model literary values

The success of human collaboration in producing the mixed works in the Park (Park, 2026) blind competition is not necessarily the result of the 41 student writings that represent professional writing or all languages.

environmental-humanities.title: Environmental Humanities
environmental-humanities.lens: More than humans, slow violence, differentiated responsibility.
environmental-humanities.intro: A source-led atlas of Environmental Humanities, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
environmental-humanities.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Environmental Humanities.
deepDive: Two of Yusoff's books place geology, slavery, ethnicization and resources in the same file, first critical of “one human world” as a way to level the black history and then track how white geology shapes the world through measurements, classifications and pricing (Yusoff, 2018; Yusoff, 2024). Liboiron goes further by defining pollution as a colonial land relationship requiring consent, responsibility and non-etilation in the methodology (Liboiron, 2021). This spectrum means that ecological criticism no longer analyses only “natural images”, but analyses infrastructure, extraction, debris, oceans, toxicity, sensors and data politics.

Application issues: Climate novels, disaster/migration narratives, marine literature, energy and mining literature, toxicity and waste, multi-species narratives, environmental transmission. Limitations: scale leaps, disaster aestheticization, indigenous knowledge as a resource, North climate theory universal, short-term experiments as an alternative to political action. The strongest design is the four parallel layers of “History Responsibility - Physical Infrastructure - Form Strategy - Reader/Community Consequences”.

Conceptual distinction. The term “global” often refers to the space of human relations made up of capital, empire and technology; the term “planetary” refers to the Earth system that is not centred on human purposes. Dipesh Chakrabarty stressed in * The climate of Histoory in a Planety Age* that both scales must be taken together: climate change requires that humanity be considered a force of species, and historical justice requires a distinction between class, empire and colonial responsibility. Core mechanisms. Literature can shift between person time, intergenerational time, geological time, turning hard-to-understood chronic damage, energy dependence and multi-species relationships into narratives; but “human life” will not remain in the grand metaphors until it enters land, mining, oceans, plantations, militarization and archival options.
coreIdeas: Planetary vs Global: Planetary scale avoids national centres, but may also level off responsibilities; research needs to map carbon capital, imperial history, class/race and species scales simultaneously (Chakrabarty, 2021).
Geology Humanities and Grail Geology: Rocks, minerals, mapping and classification are cultural-political archives, not literary backgrounds (Yusoff, 2018; Yusoff, 2024).

Blue/island/hydrography: the ocean is not a blank passage, nor is the island a climate fable; water should be read from shipping, nuclear testing, sea level, slave trade and islander knowledge (DeLoughrey, 2019).

Experienced ecological criticism (ecocricism): linking environmental narrative analysis to questionnaires, experiments, interviews, testing of whether and how narratives change perceptions, emotions and actions (Schneider-Mayerson et al., 2023). The point is whether short-term attitude tables represent long-term political behaviour.
method: Multiscale text analysis: fine reading of rhetoric/tact, with production of time lines for extraction, shipping, disasters, migration or pollution; and matching narrative time with geological/policy time.
Environmental archives: corporate reports, maps, colonial mapping, meteorological records, oral history, literature and visual materials; records of the file silence and who manufactures the data.

Empirical ecological criticism: pre-registration stimulus, random conditions, baseline attitudes, instant/delayed measurements and open reactions; distinguishing narrative transport, emotions from action, avoiding “like novels = taking climate action” (Schneider-Mayerson, etc., 2023).

Application issues: Climate novels, disaster/migration narratives, marine literature, energy and mining literature, toxicity and waste, multi-species narratives, environmental transmission. Limitations: scale leaps, disaster aestheticization, indigenous knowledge as a resource, North climate theory universal, short-term experiments as an alternative to political action. The strongest design is the four parallel layers of “History Responsibility - Physical Infrastructure - Form Strategy - Reader/Community Consequences”.
cases: Chakrabarty (Chakrabarty, 2021) |global/planetary perspective compares climate novels to how countries, species and Earth time are exchanged
DeLoughey (DeLoughey, 2019) The Man of the Islands and Empires, the Pacific Nuclear Archives, the Caribbean Hurricane Narrative, the Sea-level Visualization
Yusoff (Yusoff, 2018; Yusoff, 2024) |Black Anthropocene and radial geology mining novels, colonial mapping, geologic museum text classification politics

decolonial-global-south.title: Postcolonial, Decolonial, and Global South Studies
decolonial-global-south.era: The colonial legacy to the present.
decolonial-global-south.intro: A source-led atlas of Postcolonial, Decolonial, and Global South Studies, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
decolonial-global-south.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Postcolonial, Decolonial, and Global South Studies.
deepDive: Africa's “freedom of understanding” (estimetic freedom) emphasizes the issue not only of joining the African dimension, but of decolonizing knowledge classification, discipline and publication centres (Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2018). Ndlovu-Gatsheni advanced from critical colonial world models to re-establishing relationships in 2023 with “reworlding from the Global South” (Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2023). This echoes the methodologicalized version of China: instead of replacing Western universality with “China's specialty”, it is the production of knowledge in a complex manner in China's relations with Asia, Africa and global capitalism (Li et al., 2023).

Questions of application: Anti-colonial literature, First Nations literature, cross-linguistic writing, imperial archives, South-South circulation, curriculum/citation politics, digital cultural sovereignty. Limitations: moral labelling of terms; certification of “knowledge of the South” with the Northern Academic Evaluation System; neglect of internal oppression in the South; lack of language skills for researchers to speak out; harm caused by the disclosure of sensitive archives. The most stringent innovation criterion in this area is whether research has changed the system of evidence and relationships, rather than simply changing the object.

Conceptual distinction. Post-colonial criticism mainly analyses imperial discourse, manifestations and multiplicity; decolonization refers to colonization that continues to operate after political independence in the intellectual, labour, race and subject categories; and decolonization also involves land, sovereignty and institutional restitution in the settlement colonial context. The three are relevant, but they cannot be replaced by each other. Core mechanisms. Walter D. Mignolo and Catherine E. Walsh in *On Decoration: Concepts, Analytics, Praxis* link decolonisation organizations to concepts, analyses and practices; Linda Tuhiwai Smith's * Declonizing Methodologies: Research and Indigenous Peoples, 3rd ed.* addresses the question to whom questions are asked, who keeps the material, who explains, who benefits and who can refuse. Theoretically, therefore, rather than “reverse the classics”, the mechanisms are re-allocation of research agendas, language work, file control, signature and feedback on results. The strongest criticism.
coreIdeas: Whether the Global South is a relational location or a geographic container: It may refer to positions in the empire, capital and intellectual hierarchy, but it has internal power of the State, class, caste, ethnic group and gender. The unification of the South would leave the elite of the post-colonial countries impersonating subaltern (Ahmad, 2023; Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2023).

The sports texts are theoretical: Ahmad studied the 1970s booklet banned in the Baluchistan region of Pakistan, suggesting that anti-colonial ideas were often collective, anonymous, underground and fragmented; access to them required “anti-infrastructure” archival methods and also had to address the ethical relationship between academia and political struggle (Ahmad, 2023).

Multilingual practice: Language mixing is not irregular, but rather may be a colonial intellectual production; but the celebration of mobility cannot ignore the language punishment of schools, publications and platforms (Chaka 2020).

The struggle between colonization and “de-Westernization”: a new set of classics or local cases is only curriculum pluralism; decolonization also involves land, ownership, data, citation, translation, financing and co-governance (Smith, 2021; Tucker et al., 2012).
method: Multilingual reading and translation spectrum: preservation of original language, sound translation, translation, interpretation; non-translation of records and differences in versions, without using English translation as a transparent double (Chaka 2020; Apter, 2013).

(c) Debris file retrieval: brochures, private collections, oral transmissions, leaflets, songs, prison texts, underground periodicals and overseas copy exchange certificates; missing records are the result of violence rather than “no thought” (Ahmad, 2023).

South-South comparisons: first to determine comparable relationships (colonial regimes, ocean corridors, translation chains, platform infrastructure) and then to compare; to avoid using the Euro-American theory as a default common denominator (Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2023; Li, etc., 2023).

Quoting and system auditing: statistical theoretical sources, author location, language of publication, cost of access and research funding; quantitative results must return to content and structure, without a quota of nationality.
cases: Linda Tuhiwai Smith (Smith, 2021) | as a colonial history and indigenous project | Upgrading from ethical clearance to common governance and return of results
Magolo & Walsh (Mignolo et al., 2018) |coloniality and praxis combine conceptual history and practice cases, not just a terminological overview

Ndlovu-Gatsheni (Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2018; Ndlovu-Gatsheni, 2023) |Reworlding | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | w w w w w w w w | | | | w | w w w w w w w w w w | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

world-literature-translation.title: World Literature and Translation
world-literature-translation.era: Gold has been on the platform since.
world-literature-translation.intro: A source-led atlas of World Literature and Translation, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
world-literature-translation.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of World Literature and Translation.
deepDive: Damrosch defines world literature by the circulation and reading of works that have gone beyond their original culture (Damrosch, 2003); Apter points out that large-scale paradigms make market readability universal and require serious consideration of non-relationability, misinterpretation and geopolitical resistance (Apter, 2013); Walkowitz's “borm translation” considers translation as a contemporary fictional and formal condition (Walkowitz, 2015); Cheah rejects literature as a global commodity flow only, emphasizing the ability to start alternative times and worlds (Cheah, 2016).

The integrated orientation of the 2020s is a multi-scale world literature: countries are still useful, but need to be analysed with multilingual places, regions, oceans, islands, migration, publishing chains and digital platforms. Cambridge World Literature History uses transregional universalization, map transfer, translation, media, circulation and planetary organizations as the same methodological framework (Debjani Ganguly (ed. ), 2021); Cambridge World Literature Guide can be used to combe concepts and academic differences (Etherington et al., 2018).

Application issues: classical translation, South-South flows, novels in languages, literary awards, publishing markets, ocean/regional comparisons, global writing on digital platforms. Limitations: English-language literature is biased; sales/translation numbers are valued; translation work is ignored; translations are compared only to the original language; and “global” samples are actually from a few capitals. High-quality projects should contain at least one text chain, one institutional chain and one reader/circulation chain.
coreIdeas: Whether circulation is equal to value: translated, awarded and enrolled courses are co-screened by publishers, agents, festivals, grants and English visibility, not the “world nature” inherent in the text.

Translation is a link and a mechanism of discrimination: it can expand accessibility and can also create differential treatment through accents, identity labels and asymmetrical resources (Angermeyer, 2023).

Academic languages are not consistent with the target language: the language of expression chosen by translators is influenced by the structure of profession, periodicals and visibility; studies of English meta-studies underestimate studies of native languages (Li, 2019).

Non-translation is not a stop-to-comparison: it requires the preservation of the history, language and political friction of words, using notes, simultaneous translations and multiple translations, rather than the quick search for a single equivalent (Apter, 2013).
method: Multi-translation detail: selection of original text of the same paragraph, translation from different ages/regions, comparison of grammar, sound, cultural words, deletion, annotated and review; recording of translation sequences and editorial decisions.

Publication and by-text studies: contracts, cover, waist covers, book reviews, awards, festivals, courses and marketing materials to re-establish the institutional path of “becoming world literature”.
Multiscale comparison: The same is read separately at multilingual local, regional and global scales, identifying what is seen/covered at each scale (Debjani Ganguly (ed.), 2021).
Translation of national identity: interview interpreter, editor, agent and reader; study of remuneration, time, risk and invisible work.
cases: How the works of Damrosch (Damrosch, 2003) are rereading and rewritten in different countries, why certain works are not available.
Apter (Apter, 2013) Quantitative structure of the publishing market re-established by untranslated words, failed translation, complete language politics
Walkowitz (Walkowitz, 2015)
Cheah (Cheah, 2016) The book of how to generate a world of experience in non-capitalist time/relationship to measure the audience's acceptance of the world

affect-postcritique.title: Affect, Reading Studies, and Postcritique
affect-postcritique.intro: A source-led atlas of Affect, Reading Studies, and Postcritique, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
affect-postcritique.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Affect, Reading Studies, and Postcritique.
deepDive: Berlant's "cruel optimism" examines why people are attached to people who actually impede their prosperity and put their emotions in crisis, class and public culture (Berlant, 2011). In the 2020s, two seemingly opposite and complementary directions emerged: Felski, in order to identify, reconcile, explain the research of art attachments, asked for an explanation of how the work connects the reader with society (Felski 2020); Brinkema ' s radical formalism, in turn, refused to equate the emotional with the subjective perception of the audience, reading the logic of the nonpersonal form from the diagram, grid, rhythm, void and threshold (Brinkema, 2022).

The Fulfilling Theory Book 2 brings Black, Quer/Trans, Indigenous, Algorithmic Governance, Media Ecology and Climate Emergencies to the forefront, showing that the field has expanded from “body strength” to ethnic sensibility, trans-mediarous public, algorithmic trauma and environmental climate (Seigworth et al., 2023). Ngai's contradictory judgement of gimpick — both laborious and inefficient, outdated and over-future — connects aesthetic emotions to forms of capitalist values (Ngai, 2020).

The application problem: reading attachment, public perception, fan studies, crisis narratives, algorithmic atmosphere, aesthetic judgment. Limitations: The concept is expanding indefinitely, the body is becoming universal, researchers replace readers' feelings, the dictionaries are being moved with compassion, and any negative feelings are being romanticized into resistance. It would be useful to identify three levels of “potential emotional movement of text organization” “respondence reported by readers” “measured physical/behavioral reactions”.
coreIdeas: Whether affect and emotion are divided: pre-set affect as a pre-linguistic, generalized body strength, easily ignoring language, race, gender and history; and call everything anamotion without conscious, atmosphere and relationship analysis. Operational definitions should be clarified and not inverted (Gregg et al., 2010; Seigworth et al., 2023).

Whether the latter criticism abandons politics: not to remove ideological criticism, restorative reading and attachment research increases the causal chain of attraction, consolation, dependence and use of works; it still has to track who gets what attachment (Sedgwick, 2003; Felski, 2020).

The emotional verbs are the content or the form: mere emotional verbs miss rhythm, perspective, pause and layout; mere verbs may ignore the actual reader. Brinkema and the empirically accepted study should form a testable dialogue (Brinkema, 2022; Hugentobler et al., 2021).

Whether negative perceptions are natural resistance: shame, anger, anxiety, fatigue can be mobilized by capital and platforms; “strong feelings” cannot be called political liberation directly (Berlant, 2011; Ngai, 2020).
method: Form - Retrospect: Mark rhythm, metametry, repetition, perspective, colour, sound, graphs and proxies; how they organize the potential for feeling rather than replacing the reader's statement (Brinkema, 2022).
Reader's diary/interview: Record attachment, exclusion, rereading and life-related links during reading; analyze the path of “identify-coordinate-interpret” (Felski, 2020).

Experiment/speaker: Emotional Dictionary, EFF/Awakening Rating and Physiological Indicators need to be associated with speech models, open answers and cross-reading; irony, free and indirect quotations, historical terms are the main source of error.

(b) The emotional identity: study of the atmosphere in the classroom, the fan circle, protests, platforms and care networks; document the body and location of researchers, but do not treat self-perception as the sole evidence.
cases: Proofing (Sedgwick, 2003) Consolation, Surprise, Spelling and Survivors of Reading + Reader Interview + History of Criticism
Brutal optimism (Berlant, 2011): rising narratives, romantic comedy, labour/education commitment analysis + historical political economy
Artistic attachment (Felski 2020) Reader 's Diaries + Nationalities + Analysis of Works
Brinkema, 2022 Treadful, violent, tabulated, graphic and rhythmal, very close reading + media analysis

cognitive-empirical-aesthetics.title: Cognitive and Empirical Aesthetics
cognitive-empirical-aesthetics.lens: I'm aware of the three-point relationship between the experimental constraints and the interpretation of the three-point relationship.
cognitive-empirical-aesthetics.intro: A source-led atlas of Cognitive and Empirical Aesthetics, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
cognitive-empirical-aesthetics.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Cognitive and Empirical Aesthetics.
deepDive: The real innovation of the 2020s was not “brain scanning of literature”, but a more sophisticated connection to the manageable text form - the reading process - the experience report - behavioural/physiological indicators. Hugentobler and Lüdtke manipulated semantic condensation by nine words, “micropoly”, and in 32 final samples, high-condensation projects were more likely to form uniform concepts, were considered more understandable and preferred; the study provided a design that separated background and outlook features (Hugentobler et al., 2021). Corcoran and others by poems/prosecutions x Whether or not the 2x2 design for end-line re-evaluation is required to validate the visual concept, 16 of the 27 persons forming complete data available; poetry conditions generally show more/more attention and look back, but the sample is small (Corcoran et al., 2023).

The intercultural orientation began to incorporate the perspective, insulation and cultural psychology of translation into cognitive translation (Chen et al., 2022). Giovanelli and the Cambridge Element of 2026 combine cognitive metaphors, cognitive grammar, text world with poetry/self-ethnic aspirations, showing that cognitive poetry is also developing into practice (Giovanelli et al., 2026).

Representation nodes: Jacobs/NCPM (Freie Universität Berlin tradition) (Jacobs, 2015); Hugentobler, Lüdtke ' s experiments intersect with neurocognitive psychology (Hugentobler et al., 2021); Vision of Liverpool team visual proof of concept (Corcoran et al., 2023); cultural neurocognitive discussion among Chinese multi-university translators (Chen et al., 2022); Cambridge Elements ' synthesis of cognitive philosophies (Giovanelli et al., 2026).
coreIdeas: Concept: define “insinuation, stranger chemistry, literary, aesthetic, understanding, and common sense” separately; define text, subjective and behavioural indicators for each frame, avoiding a measurement.

Stimulant materials: Match length, frequency, metronology, subject matter, familiarity and layout; if a poem is rewritten as a essay, “poetry” and nature should be pre-tested, with all the incentives to be made public.

Samples and designs: ex ante efficacy analysis, exclusion criteria, random sequence, control conditions, language proficiency and literary experience; cross-language studies need to report bilingual background.
Measurement: different questions are answered by reading, eye motion, leather power, heart rate, EEG/fMRI; priority multilayer/mix effect model, with participants and text as random effects.
method: Stimulant materials: Match length, frequency, metronology, subject matter, familiarity and layout; if a poem is rewritten as a essay, “poetry” and nature should be pre-tested, with all the incentives to be made public.

Samples and designs: ex ante efficacy analysis, exclusion criteria, random sequence, control conditions, language proficiency and literary experience; cross-language studies need to report bilingual background.
Measurement: different questions are answered by reading, eye motion, leather power, heart rate, EEG/fMRI; priority multilayer/mix effect model, with participants and text as random effects.
Open Science: Pre-registration, irritation and analysis codes, de-marking data, effect and confidence interval, all analytical branches; small samples clearly marked “conceptual validation”.
cases: Whether or not to slow down reading for for futures and enhance aesthetic originals/minimum rewriting + eye moves + aesthetic/understanding tables + open association to rewrite unnatural; difficult words rather than foregrounds cause slowdown
Narrative predictions of manipulation of information sequences/turns when broken + memory + probability judgement (Kukkonen 2020) typologies and extreme experience

"Reverse" to "reconciled" than "reconciling" in the essay? Queues/compatible essay x endline re-evaluation, pre-registered copy (Corcoran et al., 2023) |reset, rhyme, length and participant literature experience

Translation perspective influences insinuation of the same original multiple translations + bilingual samples + perspective labeled + insinuation/memory language proficiency, translation nature, cultural familiarity (Chen et al., 2022)

gender-queer-trans.title: Queer, Trans, and Anti-Gender Politics
gender-queer-trans.intro: A source-led atlas of Queer, Trans, and Anti-Gender Politics, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
gender-queer-trans.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Queer, Trans, and Anti-Gender Politics.
deepDive: Marquis Bey has shifted Black transfers feminism from a permanent representative to a fugitive, gender radicalism and power abolition awareness approach (Bey, 2022). Malatino extends “care” from intimate virtues to mutual aid, medical care, bureaucracy and exhausted infrastructure (Malatino, 2020). Awkward-Rich points out that the Trans research has been cut in conjunction with Madness, Billness and Disability to gain the legitimacy of the system, proposing “trans malays justice” to reread literary and theoretical archives (Awkward-Rich, 2022). Stanley's “Climate of Violence” extends the focus from individual hate crimes to manageable/ungoverable life-related structural hostility (Stanley, 2021).

Global and media orientation are equally critical. Zengin presented the “violence intimacy” of Istanbul's historical national identity, analysing how police, psychiatry, family, law, funerals and transkin together form the urban world (AslıZengin, 2024). Dame-Griff has been using digital archives and media archaeology to reconstruct the trans-gender Internet since the 1980s, proving the evolution of identity terms, communities and platform infrastructure (Dame-Griff, 2023). Smilges understood the silence of the Cools as a truism of action, and resisted setting the rhetoric and visibility as the only path to liberation (Smilges, 2022).

Application issues: Cross-gender literature, Cool Child Files, Platform Community, Care, Cities, Disability/Madical Health, Gender-based Voices. Limitations: English/US Theory Centre, sample “visited” bias, research cabinets, trauma consumption, generalization of non-diverse concepts into different linguistic cultures. High-quality research should make community risk, data access and the form of results as important as theory.
coreIdeas: Identity category vs Analytical Operations: Trans is both a specific life/system category and can mean cross-cutting, transformation and relationship operations; the latter cannot eliminate the material violence to which the former is exposed (Bey, 2022; Aslı Zengin, 2024).

The double-edged sword of visible politics: archives and platforms can form a community and can trigger surveillance, cabinets, harassment and permanent preservation. Research on “discovering” historical pages does not automatically authorize the re-disclosure of personal data (Dame-Griff, 2023).

Care and regularization: Medical/family care supports life and may require a diagnostic and submissive subject; research needs to look at resources, labour and thresholds simultaneously (Malatino, 2020; AslıZengin, 2024).

Cool/cross-gender and disability: The “health, adaptation, integrity” criterion cannot be used as an emancipation criterion; the mad/crip perspective changes the understanding of narrative coherence, autonomy and repair (Awkward-Rich, 2022; Smilges, 2022).
method: Trans-of-color/cross-reading: analysis of race, class, handicap, colonization and gender without any axis as an “additional variable”.

Community archives and digital history: Wayback snapshot, BBS, mailing list, Zine and oral history cross-check; default minimum disclosure, vague and identifiable information, linking to the body of the remaining information (Dame-Griff, 2023).

National identity: Dynamic consent, pseudonym level, situational risk assessment, member verification and local language; avoiding making sex work, medical and violence details traceable files (AslıZengin, 2024).
(c) Maps of emotional/care: Record who provides emotional, information, transportation, housing, legal and medical labour, tracking depletion and resource flows (Malatino, 2020).
cases: Black Trans Feminism (Bey, 2022) Poetry, Interview, Black Feminist thought of escape/re-endorsing rather than re-identification
Trans Care (Malatino 2020) Solidarity, Crisis, Care for Work Infrastructure and Political Action
The Terrable We (Awkward-Rich, 2022) |transmasculine literature, mad/crip archive to “unsuitable” to repeat the division of disciplines
Atmospheres of Violet (Stanley, 2021) Moving from incident database to atmosphere and system

memory-trauma-platform.title: Memory, Trauma, and Platforms
memory-trauma-platform.era: The Holocaust is a memory to the platform.
memory-trauma-platform.lens: Multi-purpose memory, associated subject, media reorganization.
memory-trauma-platform.intro: A source-led atlas of Memory, Trauma, and Platforms, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
memory-trauma-platform.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Memory, Trauma, and Platforms.
deepDive: The shift in the 2020s was to view memory as a technological-platform-political economic process. Keightley believes that post-colonial memory research cannot be a medium of transmission in analog/digital technology, but rather a study of infrastructure, ownership and the environment (Keightley, 2022). Smith, Jacobsen, Annabell summed up multiple memories, methods, worlds, subjects, dynamics, time and technology with "multipleness of platform-based memory" (Smit et al., 2024). Makortykh further distinguishes between the three types of memory transmission of people - humans, humans - robots, robots - and suggests search, referral, chat agency and generation systems as memory actors (Makhortykh, 2024).

Application issues: War/colonial/disaster literature, intergenerational memory, memorials, digital heritage, social platforms, Ai history narratives. Limitations: synthesizing trauma, victimization, inducement to witness, black boxes on platforms, contamination files of synthetic content, re-transmission of hazardous materials. It is important to distinguish between the four layers of “history facts” of “memory performance” and “memory practice” of “platform”.

Conceptual distinction. The trauma study focuses on how violence experiences return with delay, fracture or physical symptoms; Marianne Hirsch, in *The General of Postmemory: Writing and Vision Culture After the Holocaust*, presents “post-rememberance” explaining how the latter generation can establish strong relationships with uninvited pasts through photographs, family narratives and silence; Michael Rothberg, * Multidirective Memoory: Remembering the Holocaust in the Age of Decoration*, rejects the interpretation of different victimization histories as zero and competition for limited public attention. Core mechanisms. Memory is constantly remediating through novels, photographs, memorials, courses, platform sequencing and generating responses, and every transfer diminishes, connects and redistributes credibility.
coreIdeas: Universalization of trauma paradigms: interpretation of colonization, war, migration as a possible shielding of local time, collective practice and continuing violence using Western clinical trauma models; need to be combined with post-colonial technological history (Keightley, 2022).

(c) Competing memory vs multi-way memory: cross-history comparisons can generate alliances or asymmetrical diversion; research seeks to track specific media, domains and power, rather than claiming “all wounds are the same” (Rothberg, 2009).

Visibility and the right to forget: Platform automatic alerts, recommendations and “supplied” photographs to change personal memory; permanent storage, deletion of failures and commercial sequencing to make forget a systemic issue (Smit et al., 2024).

Synthetic memory and AI agents: chat robots can retrieve, generate and even exchange past information between systems; the central question is not whether machines are human “rememberable”, but who sets values, corrects errors and respects the victim's obligation (Makhortykh, 2024).
method: Memory vector spectrometry: cross-family photographs of the same event, novels, memorials, textbooks, social platforms and AI response tracking; recording of each remedial deletion.
Multi-way comparison: establish physical links to the exchange/exhibit/activity/translation, re-analyze borrowing, competition, silence and alliances (Rothberg, 2009).

Oral history and post-rememberance: intergenerational interviews, photo presentations, family files; avoiding interviewing to force a full narrative of trauma, allowing silence and withdrawal (Hirsch, 2012).

Platform go-live: new account/historical account cross-check, recording recommendations, searches, automatic alarms, deletions and data downloads; date, region, language and personalized status of freeze.
cases: Post-Memorial Writing/Verror Culture (Hirsch, 2012) Concept translation and difference of the Intergenerational Trauma through image, narrative and imagination of the non-Jewish/non-European-American context
Multi-Turkish Memory (Rothberg, 2009) The Holocaust and Decolonisation Memory are not natural zero-sum institutional, linguistic and political asymmetries
The subject (Rothberg, 2019) is responsible for more than the victim/sitter. How to translate into action and institutional rehabilitation
Post-colonial memory technology (Keightley, 2022) is part of the colonial political economy. Evidence from local platforms/archivists

medical-humanities-disability.title: Medical Humanities and Disability Studies
medical-humanities-disability.era: Narrative medicine to criticalization of healthy humanities
medical-humanities-disability.intro: A source-led atlas of Medical Humanities and Disability Studies, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
medical-humanities-disability.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Medical Humanities and Disability Studies.
deepDive: New growth points in the 2020s include: healthy humanities, graphic medicine, handicapped/crip medical literature, global health narratives, care work, chronic diseases/long COVID, digital health and participatory public humanities. The Diedrich synthesis can be traced to the American Literature-Medical Spectrograph (Diedrich, 2023); the 10th edition of the Image Medicine Proclamation summing up the expansion of cartoons in caring, education, disability and marginal voices (Czerwiec et al., 2025); and Sterne combines “incapacitation” with media history, self-ethicidal and political phenomena in her own voice, hearing, fatigue, and considers “incomparable” as a universal but unequal political-physical dimension (Sterne, 2022). Cohen, from the medical “no idea” to reflect on the gap between healing and biomedical knowledge (Cohen, 2023).

Application issues: Disease/care literature, clinical communication, cartoons, disability, mental health, public health narratives, digital health. Limitations: therapeutic overstretch, patient story-tooling, privacy, doctor-centred, universalization of the North American medical system, overextension of qualitative samples. Researchers should clearly separate literature interpretation, experience knowledge and evidence of clinical effects.

Conceptual distinction. Narrative medicine organizes detailed reading, writing and clinical relationships as a care, reproduction and belonging; critical medical humanities ask how medical knowledge, systems, physical environment and body produce “diseases” and “normality”; and disability research further distinguishes between impairment, social handicap and physical-environmental relations. Rita Charon's *Narrative Medicine: Honoring the Houses of Illness* provides a systematic framework for clinical narrative capacity, but it is not a simple prescription for “reading novels and becoming one of minds”. Core mechanisms. The disease story can be a communicationable form of pain, time, diagnosis and identity, and is subject to a medical record template, insurance system, patient authority and acceptable outcome. William Viney, Felicity Callard and Angela Woods, in “Critical Medical Humanities: Embracing Environment, Taking Risksdocks”, “Crowning against humanities” is a complement to medicine: literature, clinical, technical and social structures should be integrated into causal and accountability analysis. The strongest criticism.
coreIdeas: Whether narrative good is over-suppositioned: coherence, rehabilitation, enlightenment is not the goal of all patients; debris, silence, repetition and endlessness are also of intellectual value (Woods, 2011).

Humanities are clinical tools or critical partners: if they are only justified by increasing the value of the same/satisfaction, they avoid hospital labour, insurance, race, disability and pharmaceutical power (Viney et al., 2015; Whitehead et al., 2016).

Diseases and disabilities are not the same: there are differences between the desire for treatment, identity, accessibility and anti-cure politics; research must hear the disabled person and not acquiesce in restoring “normal” (Stene, 2022).
Natural empowerment of images: comics can express time, pain and invisible symptoms, but publication, visual exposure and reader scrutiny can still simplify or consume pain (Czerwiec et al., 2025).
method: Narrative/Form Analysis: Time, Voice, Perspective, Inflection, Diagnosis, Silence, Discretion and Endurance; Avoid coding patient stories as disease “attitude”.

Narrative interviews: open questions, sequence and pause of participants, trauma-informed processes, separation of clinical and research roles; member verification does not force participants to accept the researcher ' s explanation.
Image/multi-modular analysis: grid frames, lines, colours, blank pages, text-image conflicts, body perspectives; combined with the creator process interview, as necessary (Czerwiec et al., 2025).

Critical system analysis: integration of electronic patient template, insurance classification, hospital space, waiting time, nursing work and digital health platforms into materials (Viney et al., 2015; Whitehead et al., 2016).
cases: Charon/Directology (Charron, 2006) |closer attestation, representation, ffiliation | clinically detailed and parallel medical records, subject to structural criticism
Frank (Frank, 2013) Recovery of disease, body and ethics/disturbation/exploration of narratives and their reverses
Woods (Woods 2011) The limits of narratives are silent, indefensible, refusing to recover.
Viney et al., with the Edinburgh Guidelines (Viney et al., 2015; Whitehead et al., 2016)

sound-media-material-text.title: Sound, Media, and Material Text
sound-media-material-text.lens: Media archaeology, listening systems, physical chains.
sound-media-material-text.intro: A source-led atlas of Sound, Media, and Material Text, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
sound-media-material-text.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Sound, Media, and Material Text.
deepDive: The field then completed three decentrizations. First, Stoever's “sonic color line” and “listening ear” analyzes how race is created through listening norms (Stoever, 2016); Eidsheim rejects the natural attributes of the sound, emphasizing that sound is produced by both material, training, speech and listener (Eidsheim, 2019). Second, Redrawing the Global South Voice Research has shifted technology, space and perception from the history of audio recordings in Europe and the United States to many parts of the South (Steingo et al., 2019). Third, Robinson's “hungry listening” judges settlers who use indigenous voices as resources to raise relationships and reject listening ethics (Robinson, 2020).

New targets in the 2020s include: synthetic voice/speak assistant, voice books and podcasts, voice cloning, play sounds, sound and environmental sensing, hearing impairments, interface sounds and digital archives. Faber tracks how “computer sounds” are sexified, personalized and hidden from work from Star Trek to Siri (Faber 2020); Sterne's " Capturing " puts sound technology, silence, hearing and fatigue into political phenomena (Sterne, 2022).

Questions of application: Sound literature, radio drama, poetry reading, podcasts, synthetic voice, archival recordings, electronic literature, and the ethnicization of hearing. Limitations: file silence, unavailability of equipment, restoration of hypocrisy, acoustic decision theory, unconscioned recording, sound extraction from body/place. The strongest design is the four-tier sync of the "technology chain-hearing system-text/form-specific listeners".
coreIdeas: (c) The history of media substances: recording equipment models, carriers, formats, sampling rates, compression, play chain and preservation migration; the same document is not equally priced for different players/speakers (Stene, 2012; Ernst et al., 2012).
Archives listen: combine catalogues, audio tags, production of documents, censorship, radio forms and oral history; “no audio recordings” may come from technical, power or preservation deviations.

Acoustic analysis: waveform, spectrum, high sound, intensity and rhythm can describe sound, but not based on a single spectra to judge race, sex or emotion; it needs to be combined with performance history and listening to national aspirations (Eidsheim, 2019).
Listen to national aspirations/speaks: participation in observation, sound journals, audio recordings, interviews; explicit voice consent, bystander privacy, geographical sensitivity and context.
method: (c) The history of media substances: recording equipment models, carriers, formats, sampling rates, compression, play chain and preservation migration; the same document is not equally priced for different players/speakers (Stene, 2012; Ernst et al., 2012).
Archives listen: combine catalogues, audio tags, production of documents, censorship, radio forms and oral history; “no audio recordings” may come from technical, power or preservation deviations.

Acoustic analysis: waveform, spectrum, high sound, intensity and rhythm can describe sound, but not based on a single spectra to judge race, sex or emotion; it needs to be combined with performance history and listening to national aspirations (Eidsheim, 2019).
Listen to national aspirations/speaks: participation in observation, sound journals, audio recordings, interviews; explicit voice consent, bystander privacy, geographical sensitivity and context.
cases: Technology/Format History (Sterne, 2003; Sterne, 2012) compares records, radio, MP3, audio books to change literary voices and attention
Ethnicized listeners to the |Stoever, Eidsheim (Stoever, 2016; Eidsheim, 2019) |s dialects, voice-based phonographs, court/radio lines
Global Southern Voice, Steingo, Sykes, eds. (Steingo et al., 2019) From the local technology network and hearing, the European and American studios are not the baseline.
The First Nations listen to the ethical Robinson (Robinson 2020) file return, restricted access, undisclosed voice of rituals

crosscutting-frontiers.intro: A source-led atlas of Crosscutting Frontiers, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
crosscutting-frontiers.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of Crosscutting Frontiers.
deepDive: Literary objects can be re-presented along the following chains: creation and collaboration of a version, code and rights attribution, choice of label, recommendation and reader work, translation and migration, teaching, archives and public use, community governance, maintenance and withdrawal. Each link in the chain should ask four questions: who has the right to decide and interpret; which jobs are paid, signed or absorbed free of charge; how laws, cultural agreements and technological infrastructure can be changed to researchable materials; and whether the chain of evidence remains in place after project liquidation, platform closure or community withdrawal of permission.

Simone Murray, in Digital Literature Fields (* The Digital Library: Reening, Writer, and Selling Books in the Internet Era*, 2018), placed author's online image, digital marketing, literary festival, web book review and reading discussions in the same domain; Sarah Brouillette, Literature and Creative Economy (*Literature and the Creative Economy*, 2014), explained how the image of author “free, flexible, self-expression” fits into unstable labour and cultural policies. Together, they change the research unit: the text type, length, cover, frequency of updates and even the pace of narratives of the work may be formed in conjunction with the structure of the platform ' s classification, contract, marketing and returns.

Melanie Walsh and Maria Antoniak, The Goodreads “Classics”: A Popular Standard of Readers, Amazon, and Crowdsourced Amateur Critical, 2021) analysed over 120,000 comments, showing that the “classic” of the user-playing exhibition also carries the platform's demographic structure and its orthodox bias. A replicable research design should combine the identity of the same work in the course catalogue, the publisher case, the Goodreads label, the star review and the recommended page, and consider the commentator's interpretation work as a platform-translable data rather than a natural and public “readers' voice”.
coreIdeas: Text to public consequences: a complete research chain
Publications, platforms and literary work: values are not added after publication Objects
Native intellectual sovereignty: Accessible does not amount to arbitrary use
Copyright, text and data mining: law is also an cognitive variable
method: The eight above-mentioned directions share a judgement that the “evidence” of literary research is not only the text's content but also the relationship that enables it to emerge, circulate, be understood and continue. The study plan should include at least six additional fields: labour and signature, jurisdiction and licensing, platforms and versions, translation and migration, community decision-making, maintenance and exit.
Sets a scale correspondence between reading, computing or experiment.
The interpretation of constraints is verified by case, reverse and triangulation.
Open uncertainty, ethical boundaries and the path to recovery.
cases: Publications, Platforms and Literature Labour Editors, Contracts, Labels, Recommendations, Commentaries, Authors and Free Readers, Literature Sociology + Platform Visiting + Text Reading + Work Interview Interface Changes, Deletements, Robotic Reviews, Platform Ownership

The title, authority, cultural agreement, reuse and withdrawal of the native intellectual sovereignty + authority modelling + archives | open access to Indigenous knowledge is not automatically legitimate

Copyright and text mining language acquisition, jurisdiction, retention of rights, derivative data | legal text analysis + language passport + review path | not to push single national rules out of the world

Interactive narratives and game typologies, branches, interfaces, state variables, replaying the difference tidling process + running the screen + version saves the tiding work is not a fixed sequence

region-north-america.title: North American Research Field
region-north-america.era: The way to rebuild in crisis
region-north-america.intro: A source-led atlas of North American Research Field, preserving the complete Chinese research chapter while mapping its evidence, methods, debates, and regional cases.
region-north-america.door: Open this volume to enter the evidence world of North American Research Field.
deepDive: The contribution of Stanford Literary Lab is not to provide a larger sample of readings, but to tear literary interpretations down into mutually verifiable steps: building a library, defining features, comparing distributions, searching for abnormal values, returning to text, and modifying models. The Toronto Digital Dostoevsky is more than an illustration: it has only five ministerial and two mid-parts, but uses structured labels and NLPs to track speech, space, time, emotion and fiction; “calculation” is not necessarily the same as mass, but can be small text that is marked with high density. (Digital Dostoevsky Project Team, n.d.)

Replicable research design: first create paragraph/person/narrative level TEI for 30-50 inter-linguistic novels; then model with interpretable features; blindly comment on the anomaly paragraphs by researchers in different languages; and disagree on consistency and interpretation in the final report. The risk is that copyright prevents the publication of language material, that English terminologists are misused in other languages, and that team work is signed only to PI. More than 40 DH projects in Toronto show that healthier ecology must recognize archives, indigenous knowledge, gender, map and literature calculations, rather than shrinking DH to NLP. (Digital Humanities Network, University of Toronto, n.d.)

The real innovation of the study is not to prove that the model will be endorsed, but to transform memory deviations into cultural analysis of effectiveness. Models that appear to be accurate in their judgement of “literacy history” will systematically reward network visibility if they are trained in popular, copyrighted works and less in small languages and marginal publications. The 70 years of Output's text file also shows that research cannot use post-2022 chat models as a machine to write the whole history; the different stages of the rule system, the combination program, the statistical model and the large model need to be analysed separately. (Chang et al., 2023; Bertram et al., 2024)
coreIdeas: From model outputs to training history audits. Large models should not be viewed as backgroundless readers. Speak, Memoory extrapolates from members of 571 novels whose names were deformed from 1749-2020, that the level of memory is found to be related to the replicability of the network; the model is better off in the “books remembered” when it performs the task of publishing the year, narrative length, etc., and thus creates a false methodological effect.

From one person to a team experiment, but retain a debatable chain of interpretation. The Stanford model of a laboratory combines text selection, modelling, visualization, abnormality reading back and short-text validation; Digital Dostoevsky uses TEI/NLP to study character speech, space, time, emotion and fiction, and is suitable for displaying the cycle of "Experience - Near-reading Validation - Text Encoding back".

From the "fair variables" to the history of power. Data feminism requires not just an increase in female samples, but rather a definition of classification, who bears the burden of maintaining labour, who is visually harmed; Chun, with retroactive relevance and homophily eugenic/separation history, suggests that model deviations may come from the default structure of the method rather than from mere dirty data. (D'Ignazio et al. 2020; Chun, 2021)

From research on disability to research on disability. Crip Autoship considers time, repetition, description, collaboration, care and accessible formats as conditions for knowledge production; Sterne ' s political modulation rejects transparent, self-contained and complete research subjects, allowing uncertainty, assistive technology and physical experience to enter methodological descriptions. (Milles et al., 2023; Sterne, 2022)
method: North American ecology is made of three nodes. The first is the Department of Literature - the Computation Laboratory: Stanford Literary Lab, which uses the computational criticism organization as a collaborative experiment and a short “Pamphlet” result, and the current project has extended to identity evolution, cross-linguistics, youth literature and narrative space.

From model outputs to training history audits. Large models should not be viewed as backgroundless readers. Speak, Memoory extrapolates from members of 571 novels whose names were deformed from 1749-2020, that the level of memory is found to be related to the replicability of the network; the model is better off in the “books remembered” when it performs the task of publishing the year, narrative length, etc., and thus creates a false methodological effect.

From one person to a team experiment, but retain a debatable chain of interpretation. The Stanford model of a laboratory combines text selection, modelling, visualization, abnormality reading back and short-text validation; Digital Dostoevsky uses TEI/NLP to study character speech, space, time, emotion and fiction, and is suitable for displaying the cycle of "Experience - Near-reading Validation - Text Encoding back".

From the "fair variables" to the history of power. Data feminism requires not just an increase in female samples, but rather a definition of classification, who bears the burden of maintaining labour, who is visually harmed; Chun, with retroactive relevance and homophily eugenic/separation history, suggests that model deviations may come from the default structure of the method rather than from mere dirty data. (D'Ignazio et al. 2020; Chun, 2021)
cases: Case A: Laboratory does not amount to "Automated Reading" - Triangular Validation for Stanford and Toronto
Case B: Speak, Memoory - Generating AI deprived literary measurements of the Clean Test Set
Case C: The “hearing” chain of narrative medicine-writers with disabilities-sounding research
Case D: Black file awareness theory and double-directional validation of publishing sociology
